signals.title: ILO Employment and Social Trends 2026: Global Jobs Gap Reaches 408 Million

content.abstract: ILO flagship report reveals global jobs gap of 408 million, with 2.1 billion workers in informal employment. Youth unemployment at 12.4%, 260 million young people NEET. Women 24.2% less likely to participate in labor force. Progress in job quality has stalled despite stable headline unemployment at 4.9%.

signals.discovery_query: social inequality labor migration demographic shifts 2026
signals.classification_rationale: Foundational labor market data revealing structural inequality persistence — 408M jobs gap signals systemic workforce crisis
signals.impact_analysis: Very high: defines the global social landscape with concrete inequality metrics affecting billions

signals.title: Climate vs. Biodiversity: Global Study Maps Forestation Dilemma — 13% Overlap Between CDR Projects and Biodiversity Hotspots

content.abstract: Nature Climate Change study finds 13% of globally important biodiversity-rich land overlaps with areas earmarked for carbon dioxide removal projects. Avoiding biodiversity hotspots entirely would reduce available CDR area by over 50% by 2050. Geographic disparity: models designate significantly more land for CDR in Global South vs North, raising equity concerns.

signals.discovery_query: climate change biodiversity environmental policy breakthroughs March 2026
signals.classification_rationale: Environmental — critical tension between two planetary boundaries (climate and biodiversity), with equity dimensions
signals.impact_analysis: High: quantifies the trade-off between climate mitigation and biodiversity protection, affecting global environmental policy

signals.title: 2026: Year at the Crossroads for Global Data Protection and Privacy — EU E-Evidence Regulation Takes Effect

content.abstract: EU E-Evidence regulation comes into force in 2026, allowing criminal authorities to obtain data directly from service providers in other countries. China Cybersecurity Law amendments effective January 2026 bring AI governance within scope of major national law. 82% of organizations concerned about cross-border investigations in 2026.

signals.discovery_query: geopolitics regulation international law policy changes March 2026
signals.classification_rationale: Political — regulatory convergence on data governance with cross-border enforcement mechanisms
signals.impact_analysis: High: cross-border data governance framework reshaping digital economy rules globally

signals.title: Two Types of AI Existential Risk: Decisive and Accumulative — New Philosophical Framework

content.abstract: New philosophical framework distinguishes between decisive existential risk (sudden catastrophic events) and accumulative existential risk (gradual erosion of human meaning, autonomy, and values). Risk fragmentation problem identified: distinct approaches to conceptualizing AI risk fail to provide complete coverage.

signals.discovery_query: AI ethics values meaning existential risk governance spiritual wellbeing 2026
signals.classification_rationale: Spiritual/Ethics — paradigm-shifting framework on meaning erosion as existential risk, beyond physical extinction
signals.impact_analysis: High novelty: reframes AI existential risk debate to include gradual loss of human meaning and autonomy

signals.title: March 2026 Economic Update: US-Iran Conflict Ripples, Trade Fragmentation, and EM Institutional Divergence

content.abstract: March 2026 economic analysis: US-Israel-Iran conflict impacting energy prices and inflation expectations. Global financial system now operates within fragmented institutional architecture with competing settlement systems. Emerging markets must navigate by institutional positioning rather than macro fundamentals. USMCA review due July 2026.

signals.discovery_query: global economic policy shifts emerging markets March 2026
signals.classification_rationale: Economic — geopolitical fragmentation creating new financial architecture with immediate market impacts
signals.impact_analysis: High urgency: active conflict affecting energy markets, plus structural shift in how emerging markets navigate global finance
